summary: Iteration 5: content expansion 100→240 questions (30 per category × 8) + backend MongoDB $sample randomization. Updated EXPECTED_COUNTS in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py to all 30s, fixed test_premium_user_gets_up_to_20 (10→20 expected), added 3 new randomization tests (free 5-of-30, premium 20-of-30, full-pool coverage via aggregation), added test_challenge_snapshot_randomized, and made test_participate_perfect_score robust against random subsets (it now collects all 30 correct_indexes across multiple calls before mapping). Pytest result: 54/54 PASSED (was 50/50). Frontend: visiting /app/quiz/chansons four times in a row produced 4 DIFFERENT first questions — true randomization confirmed end-to-end. UI shows 'Question 1 / 20' for admin (premium). All 8 category cards still render on Landing.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend get_questions() correctly uses $sample aggregation: confirmed truly random per call by collecting 5 free-tier sets (4-5 distinct) and 5 premium-tier sets (4-5 distinct) for /api/categories/chansons/questions.

Full-pool coverage test (test_total_pool_30_per_category) verifies that across 15 successive premium calls per category, exactly 30 unique question IDs are observed for each of the 8 categories — confirming the seed loaded all 240 questions and no duplicate IDs.

Challenge snapshot ($sample on creation) is also random: 3 successive POST /api/challenges with category_id=chansons num_questions=10 produced at least 2 distinct snapshots (verified by test_challenge_snapshot_randomized).

Frontend QuizPlayer.jsx fetches /api/categories/{id}/questions on mount, so each navigation to /app/quiz/{id} triggers a fresh $sample — verified visually with 4 different first questions on 4 successive visits to /app/quiz/chansons as admin.

Note (pre-existing, content-quality only): q40_3 (Indochine 'Aventurier' — actually correct as worded in current seed) and option text mismatches reported in iteration_4 are unchanged and not blockers.
Suggestion: get_questions() in server.py could benefit from an LRU cache around the categories metadata lookup (small win); not required.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Landing 8 cards + premium quiz shows 20 questions + 4/4 distinct first questions across 4 successive visits = randomization confirmed)

seed_data_creation: Each pytest run still registers ~4 throw-away free users (qa.test.*, qa.reg.*, qa.dup.*) and creates several challenges (chansons 4q, culture-40-ans 10q, cinema 4q, plus 3 chansons 10q for snapshot-randomization test). No cleanup needed; data isolated by timestamped emails.
context_for_next_testing_agent: EXPECTED_COUNTS is now all 30s. Pool-coverage test asserts exactly 30 unique IDs per category — if you grow the pool again past 30, update both EXPECTED_COUNTS and the assertion in test_total_pool_30_per_category. The test_participate_perfect_score test now iterates up to 20 calls of /api/categories/cinema/questions to build a complete answer map; this is required because $sample only returns a 20-of-30 subset per call. If $sample is ever replaced with a deterministic strategy, this loop can be simplified.
